audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 John text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for trust/love/commands, overcoming, water/blood/Spirit witness, life in Son, confidence, sin leading/not leading to death, idols. Comma Johanneum variant flagged.

verses.restored: Everyone trusting that Jesus is the Messiah has been born from the Divine, and everyone loving the One who begot loves the one begotten from him.
In this we know that we love the children of the Divine: whenever we love the Divine and do his commands.
For this is the love of the Divine, that we keep his commands; and his commands are not heavy.
Because everyone born from the Divine overcomes the world-system; and this is the victory that overcame the world-system: our trust.
And who is the one overcoming the world-system except the one trusting that Jesus is the Son of the Divine?
This is the One coming through water and blood, Jesus Messiah; not in the water only, but in the water and in the blood. And the Spirit is the One testifying, because the Spirit is truth.
For there are three testifying:
the Spirit and the water and the blood, and the three are into the one.
If we receive human witness, the witness of the Divine is greater, because this is the witness of the Divine, that he has testified concerning his Son.

The one trusting in the Son of the Divine has the witness in oneself; the one not trusting the Divine has made him liar, because one has not trusted in the witness that the Divine has testified concerning his Son.
And this is the witness: that the Divine gave us age-long life, and this life is in his Son.
The one having the Son has the life; the one not having the Son of the Divine does not have the life.
These things I wrote to you so that you may know that you have age-long life, to those trusting in the name of the Son of the Divine.
And this is the boldness that we have toward him: that if we ask anything according to his will, he hears us.
And if we know that he hears us, whatever we ask, we know that we have the requests that we have asked from him.

If anyone sees one's sibling sinning/misaligning a sin/misalignment not toward death, one will ask, and he will give life to that one, to those sinning/misaligning not toward death. There is sin/misalignment toward death; concerning that I do not say that one should ask.
Every injustice/unrighteousness is sin/misalignment, and there is sin/misalignment not toward death.
We know that everyone born from the Divine does not keep sinning/misaligning, but the One born from the Divine keeps that one, and the harmful one does not touch that one.
We know that we are from the Divine, and the whole world-system lies in the harmful one.

And we know that the Son of the Divine has come and has given us understanding, so that we may know the True One; and we are in the True One, in his Son Jesus Messiah. This is the true Divine and age-long life.
Little children, guard yourselves from idols.

translation_notes: The later Comma Johanneum is not included in the restored text; verses 7-8 follow the shorter Greek witness: Spirit, water, blood.
Commands are not heavy because they are love-shaped, not domination-shaped.
Sin toward death is left deliberately cautious and not overdefined.
